_structure: Game state data model. Defines all persistent and transient state required for the core loop. Organized by scope: global (across runs), run-state (per roguelike run), character-state (per unit).

roster.description: All characters ever created. Includes dead characters for genealogy.

genes.description: Inherited genetic traits. Determines base card pool.

unlocked_training_options.description: Global pool of trainings discovered in any run. Persists when teacher retires.

fields.name: training name
fields.cards_unlocked: array of card IDs this training grants

global_resources.description: Currency and materials shared across runs. Spent on full childrearing.

fields.rare_materials: optional, if special breeding unlocks exist

scope_run.description: Resets when a roguelike run ends (either victory or total extinction). Tracks current campaign state.

active_roster.description: Characters currently alive and available. Subset of global roster.

run_resources.description: Accumulated during this run, spent on repairs/breeding/training.

fields.mates_encountered: array of mate objects available for breeding this run

breeding_queue.description: Children scheduled to be born and trained before next campaign.

fields.training_plan: array of training IDs to teach during growing-up

equipment_state.description: Each character's current gear and durability.

scope_character.description: Per-unit state. Combination of persistent (roster) and transient (in-campaign).

card_pool.description: Combat cards available for deck drawing. Built from: genes + training + equipment.

fields.gene_cards: array of card IDs from inherited genes (full child has all parent genes, cheap child has subset)
fields.training_cards: array of card IDs from received training
fields.equipment_cards: array of card IDs from equipped items (temporary, changes with loadout)

deck.description: Active deck in combat. Subset of card_pool, drawn at battle start.

fields.current_deck: array of card IDs in shuffled order
fields.reshuffle_threshold: when discard_pile size reaches this, reshuffle (e.g., 70% of deck)

teacher_status.description: If appointed as teacher, tracks which training they can teach and to whom.

fields.taught_training_ids: array of training IDs they know (can teach to children)
fields.current_student_ids: array of children currently being taught

scope_combat.description: Ephemeral state during a single battle. Discarded after battle ends.

fields.battle_state: array of unit states (position, HP, status effects, etc.)

design_decisions.decision: Cheap childrearing produces subset of parent genes
design_decisions.rationale: Creates meaningful breeding strategy: do I invest gold for full genetic expression, or breed cheap for roster depth?

design_decisions.decision: Teachers are appointments, not magical inheritance
design_decisions.rationale: Preserves trained population under permadeath pressure. Teachers can retire but still teach until death.
Makes equipment a resource sink competing with breeding/training. Encourages rotating active roster.

open_questions: How are genes numerically inherited? (Both parents → blend? One parent dominant per trait? Random draw?)
Does cheap childrearing reduce gene *count* or gene *potency*? (e.g., 50% of genes vs. 100% at half strength)
What's the max_age at retirement? (Should scale with roguelike difficulty?)
